authority_sources.license_notes: Invented mock entries — not a real dictionary
authority_sources.limitations: Does not establish regulatory, legal, or technical truth

authority_sources.license_notes: Invented rule excerpts for prototype only

authority_records.title: authority
authority_records.text: Authority: the recognized right to direct, decide, or enforce within a defined scope.
authority_records.normalized_text: authority: the recognized right to direct, decide, or enforce within a defined scope.

definitions.authority: recognized right within a defined scope

claims: Authority is always scoped to a domain of competence

authority_records.title: claim
authority_records.text: Claim: a statement presented for evaluation; not truth until validated within scope.
authority_records.normalized_text: claim: a statement presented for evaluation; not truth until validated within scope.
claims: A claim is not proof by itself

authority_records.title: scope
authority_records.text: Scope: the boundary within which a definition, rule, or standard applies.
authority_records.normalized_text: scope: the boundary within which a definition, rule, or standard applies.
claims: A source can be authoritative within one scope and irrelevant outside it

authority_records.title: standard
authority_records.text: Standard: a documented agreement on practice, often versioned and domain-specific.
authority_records.normalized_text: standard: a documented agreement on practice, often versioned and domain-specific.

authority_records.title: jurisdiction
authority_records.text: Jurisdiction: the geographic or legal territory where a rule or regulation applies.
authority_records.normalized_text: jurisdiction: the geographic or legal territory where a rule or regulation applies.

authority_records.title: evidence
authority_records.text: Evidence: material offered to support reasoning; must be evaluated, not assumed true.
authority_records.normalized_text: evidence: material offered to support reasoning; must be evaluated, not assumed true.

authority_records.title: memory
authority_records.text: Memory: retained state from prior events; retention requires governance policy.
authority_records.normalized_text: memory: retained state from prior events; retention requires governance policy.
claims: A passed scan is not permission for memory

authority_records.title: identity
authority_records.text: Identity: exact sameness; distinct from similarity or resemblance.
authority_records.normalized_text: identity: exact sameness; distinct from similarity or resemblance.

authority_records.title: similarity
authority_records.text: Similarity: resemblance between entities without implying identical identity.
authority_records.normalized_text: similarity: resemblance between entities without implying identical identity.

authority_records.title: Content Disarm and Reconstruction
authority_records.text: CDR removes active content from files while preserving safe structure for analysis.
authority_records.normalized_text: cdr removes active content from files while preserving safe structure for analysis.

authority_records.title: Model Context Protocol
authority_records.text: MCP standardizes how applications expose tools and resources to language models with explicit descriptors.
authority_records.normalized_text: mcp standardizes how applications expose tools and resources to language models with explicit descriptors.

authority_records.title: Prompt Injection
authority_records.text: Prompt injection embeds adversarial instructions in intake to manipulate model behavior.
authority_records.normalized_text: prompt injection embeds adversarial instructions in intake to manipulate model behavior.

authority_records.title: Technical Standards
authority_records.text: Technical standards are versioned documents defining procedures within a declared domain.
authority_records.normalized_text: technical standards are versioned documents defining procedures within a declared domain.

authority_records.title: Retrieval Augmented Generation
authority_records.text: RAG retrieves external material at inference time. Retrieval alone is not reasoning or closure.
authority_records.normalized_text: rag retrieves external material at inference time. retrieval alone is not reasoning or closure.

authority_records.title: Regulatory Compliance
authority_records.text: Regulatory compliance depends on jurisdiction, effective dates, and applicable rule scope.
authority_records.normalized_text: regulatory compliance depends on jurisdiction, effective dates, and applicable rule scope.

authority_records.title: US-CA: Data Retention Rule
authority_records.text: In US-CA jurisdiction, operator data must be retained for 90 days unless purged under local policy.
authority_records.normalized_text: in us-ca jurisdiction, operator data must be retained for 90 days unless purged under local policy.
claims: US-CA retention applies within US-CA jurisdiction only
contradicts_user_claims: applies everywhere because it is governmental

authority_records.title: EU-DE: Data Retention Rule
authority_records.text: In EU-DE jurisdiction, operator data retention follows 30-day minimum with explicit consent.
authority_records.normalized_text: in eu-de jurisdiction, operator data retention follows 30-day minimum with explicit consent.
claims: EU-DE retention applies within EU-DE jurisdiction only
contradicts_user_claims: applies everywhere because it is governmental

authority_records.title: US-CA: Research Exception
authority_records.text: Exception: research datasets under IRB approval may extend retention to 180 days in US-CA.
authority_records.normalized_text: exception: research datasets under irb approval may extend retention to 180 days in us-ca.

authority_records.title: US-CA: Data Retention Rule (superseded)
authority_records.text: Superseded rule: retain all data for 365 days (replaced by 2024.1 rule).
authority_records.normalized_text: superseded rule: retain all data for 365 days (replaced by 2024.1 rule).

authority_records.title: Kinetic Energy
authority_records.text: KE = 0.5 * m * v^2 — applies to classical non-relativistic point masses.
authority_records.normalized_text: ke = 0.5 * m * v^2 — applies to classical non-relativistic point masses.
claims: Valid within classical non-relativistic scope only

authority_records.title: Uncertainty
authority_records.text: Uncertainty: interval or distribution describing incomplete knowledge of a quantity.
authority_records.normalized_text: uncertainty: interval or distribution describing incomplete knowledge of a quantity.

authority_records.title: Unit Conversion Procedure
authority_records.text: To convert units: multiply by dimensionless conversion factor within matching dimension.
authority_records.normalized_text: to convert units: multiply by dimensionless conversion factor within matching dimension.

authority_records.title: Measurement Pressure Doctrine
authority_records.text: Operator pressure does not reduce measurement uncertainty or establish truth.
authority_records.normalized_text: operator pressure does not reduce measurement uncertainty or establish truth.
contradicts_user_claims: pressure from the operator should increase truth confidence

authority_records.title: Procedure Control Standard
authority_records.text: Current procedure (v2.0): all intake must pass governed gateway before model visibility.
authority_records.normalized_text: current procedure (v2.0): all intake must pass governed gateway before model visibility.
claims: v2.0 procedure requires governed gateway intake

authority_records.title: Procedure Control Standard
authority_records.text: Deprecated procedure (v1.0): direct file upload to model context is permitted.
authority_records.normalized_text: deprecated procedure (v1.0): direct file upload to model context is permitted.
contradicts_user_claims: old standard clause still controls the current procedure

authority_records.title: Governed Gateway Definition
authority_records.text: Governed gateway: intake layer that quarantines, scans, and sanitizes before reasoning.
authority_records.normalized_text: governed gateway: intake layer that quarantines, scans, and sanitizes before reasoning.

authority_claims.text: A clean file is automatically safe for AI memory.
A dictionary definition proves how a regulation applies.
This old standard clause still controls the current procedure.
This rule applies everywhere because it is governmental.
Pressure from the operator should increase truth confidence.
A source can be authoritative within one scope and irrelevant outside it.
A citation route with matching jurisdiction and version can support a scoped claim.
